body:
### Problem

I am running neovim 0.12.1 on Chimera Linux.

I am trying to open a .lua file.

The command I am running is:
`nvim --clean test.lua`

The `NVIM_APPNAME` environment variable is set in order to run neovim in a totally clean context. My default config has lots of plugins etc.

I get the following error:

```
Error in BufReadPost Autocommands for "*":                                                                      
Lua callback: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/filetype.lua:28: BufReadPost Autocommands for "*"..FileType Autocommands f
or "*"..function <SNR>1_LoadFTPlugin[20]..script /usr/share/nvim/runtime/ftplugin/lua.lua: Vim(runtime):E5113: L
ua chunk: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:279: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/treesi
tter/query.lua:374: Query error at 74:3. Invalid field name "operator":                                         
  operator: _ @operator)                                                                                        
  ^                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                
stack traceback:                                                                                                
        [C]: in function 'error'                                                                                
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:279: in function 'get_query'                 
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:109: in function 'set_conceal_lines'         
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:157: in function 'new'                       
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter.lua:450: in function 'start'                                 
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/ftplugin/lua.lua:2: in main chunk                                               
        [C]: in function 'nvim_cmd'                                                                             
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/filetype.lua:28: in function </usr/share/nvim/runtime/filetype.lua:27>          
        [C]: in function 'pcall'                                                                                
        vim/_core/shared.lua: in function <vim/_core/shared.lua:0>                                              
        [C]: in function '_with'                                                                                
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/filetype.lua:27: in function </usr/share/nvim/runtime/filetype.lua:10>          
stack traceback:                                                                                                
        [C]: in function '_with'                                                                                
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/filetype.lua:27: in function </usr/share/nvim/runtime/filetype.lua:10>
```

The problem appears specific to Chimera Linux, unfortunately. I also tested 0.12.1 and 0.12.2 on Fedora and neither of them reproduce the error.

### Steps to reproduce

Use Chimera Linux, perhaps using a distrobox:
`distrobox create --image chimeralinux/chimera`

Create an empty file with ".lua" extension:
`touch test.lua`

Open with nvim:
`nvim test.lua`

### Expected behavior

No error

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

0.12.1

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

no, vim 9.1

### Operating system/version

Chimera Linux

### Terminal name/version

tmux

### $TERM environment variable

tmux-256color

### Installation

Package manager

comment_data.body:
query.lua:374: Query error at 74:3. Invalid field name "operator"

This indicates that Chimera Linux is bundling the wrong treesitter parsers/queries.

For future reference is there a canonical place to look to know what version of tree-sitter and grammars are required for a particular Neovim release? BUILD.md says to look at Debian/Homebrew but feels a little non-authoritative when we're trying to update our own distro package. Files in `cmake.deps/cmake` also don't appear to contain version information.

**Edit:** I think I found the answer: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/cmake.deps/deps.txt

Although still not sure where tree-sitter version is captured, perhaps implied by the grammars?

labels.description: Needs a third-party / external change or fix
labels.name: status:blocked-external

title: Error when opening lua files: `Query error at 74:3. Invalid field name "operator"`
